reasoning_model_1: The generated audio by Model 1 (00:00-00:02) demonstrates excellent Content Accuracy, strictly adhering to the user's script '别担心，一切都会好起来的。' without any additions or omissions. In terms of the core paralinguistic feature '音高' (Pitch), the model effectively lowers the pitch to convey a comforting and steady tone as requested ('请用低音说'). The low pitch is consistent throughout the short sentence, creating a warm and reassuring atmosphere that aligns perfectly with the semantic meaning of 'don't worry'. The prosody is natural and free from artifacts.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 (00:00-00:07) exhibits a critical failure in Content Accuracy. While it begins with the target sentence '别担心，一切都会好起来的', it proceeds to hallucinate and read aloud a poetic description of the voice itself ('声音沉稳如大地回响...'), which was likely intended as a style prompt or internal descriptor rather than speech content. This severe content error violates the instruction to 'read this sentence'. Consequently, the evaluation of '音高' (Pitch) is compromised; while the model might attempt a lower pitch, the shift into a narrative reading of the style description ('sound is calm like the earth...') breaks the immersion and character intent. The inclusion of the stage directions makes the audio completely incorrect for the intended purpose.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the content instructions perfectly and applied the requested 'low pitch' feature to the specific sentence provided. Model 2 failed significantly by hallucinating and reading out loud a description of the voice style (acting out the stage directions/metadata) instead of just speaking the dialogue. This makes Model 2's audio unusable, regardless of the acoustic quality of the pitch, as it violates the core content constraints. Model 1 provides a concise, accurate, and stylistically correct response.
